brand.name: Pain Relief Products / Healed by Farm
brand.vertical: Health & Wellness — DTC CBD / Natural Supplement

summary: Pain Relief Products, founder John Rankin's 'Healed by Farm' brand out of Knoxville TN, sells a single hero product: a full-spectrum CBD tincture ('Wellness in a Bottle,' 163 mL / 3,300 mg CBD in organic olive oil) for $99.95 as a one-time purchase. It serves health-conscious adults 35-65+ with chronic pain, joint discomfort, inflammation, or poor sleep who prefer natural, drug-free alternatives to pharmaceuticals. Revenue is purely transactional with no subscription, so re-order cadence and post-purchase activation are the core retention levers.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation — get the new buyer to consistent daily dosing and their first noticeable relief or sleep improvement within 7-14 days, before they give up.
campaigns.trigger: First purchase / order confirmed. Send email 1 immediately after the order confirmation.
campaigns.segment: All first-time tincture buyers; branch later by primary goal (pain relief vs. sleep) if captured.
campaigns.rationale: This brand is entirely self-serve with no dosing guidance — buyers who don't know how much to take, when, or how long it takes to work will churn before the aha moment ever arrives. Setting the 7-14 day expectation is the single highest-leverage fix for a single-SKU, no-subscription model.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome from John, set the dosing routine and the 7-14 day timeline, and deliver one quick win: take your first dose tonight.
emails.subject: Your Wellness in a Bottle is on its way — start here
emails.preview: How much to take, when to take it, and what the first two weeks feel like.
keyPoints: Personal welcome from John Rankin tying back to his own farm-to-health story so the buyer trusts the source
Simple daily dosing routine: how many drops, taken with food, same time each day for consistency
Set the honest expectation — most people notice less pain or better sleep within 7-14 days of daily use, so don't stop after a few days
One first action tonight: take your first dose and note how you slept
Real-person reply-to invitation, plus required FDA disclaimer ('not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease')

campaigns.name: Re-Order Nudge & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect repeat revenue by prompting a refill before the bottle runs out, and seed multi-bottle / future auto-ship as the expansion path.
campaigns.trigger: Day 25-28 after the prior order ships (a 163 mL bottle is a ~30-45 day supply; the re-order window is day 25-35).
campaigns.segment: Buyers ~25-28 days post-purchase with no second order yet; branch healthy (felt results) vs. unsure.
campaigns.rationale: With no subscription and a single SKU, revenue bleeds silently at day 30-35 when buyers simply forget to reorder. A well-timed refill nudge — substituting for a renewal flow — is the most direct retention play this brand has.

emails.purpose: Check in on how they're feeling, remind them not to break the daily streak they've built, and make refilling a one-tap action before they run dry.
emails.subject: Running low? Don't lose the progress you've made
emails.preview: Most people feel the difference by week 4 — keep it going with a fresh bottle.
keyPoints: 'How are you feeling?' check-in that reframes the refill around the relief and sleep they've started to notice
Remind them consistency is what makes CBD work — stopping at week 4 undoes the routine they built
Social proof from customers who reported their best results around the 30-day mark
Low-friction one-click reorder with free shipping, plus a soft mention of buying two bottles to never run out
FDA disclaimer and a real reply-to for dosing questions

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture (Book & Podcast Audience)
campaigns.objective: Move a cold lead from the book/podcast/blog audience toward their first tincture purchase by building trust and answering the 'does it actually work?' question over a multi-week cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Email opt-in from a content asset — downloading a chapter of 'Healed by Farm,' subscribing after the podcast, or a blog newsletter signup.
campaigns.segment: Non-buyers who entered via content; branch by topic interest (chronic pain vs. sleep vs. ancestral wellness).
campaigns.rationale: The book and podcast pull in exactly the natural-health audience this brand needs, but they aren't visibly funneled into email — so warm, high-intent leads leak out of the top of the funnel. Nurturing them addresses the category's #1 barrier (quality/efficacy skepticism) before the $100 ask.

emails.purpose: Deliver the asset they signed up for and frame the real problem — being stuck choosing between side-effect-heavy painkillers and overhyped CBD — through John's story.
emails.subject: Why John walked away from the pill bottle
emails.preview: The farm-sourced approach behind Healed by Farm — and what makes CBD actually work.
keyPoints: Deliver the promised content (book chapter / podcast episode / guide) right up top
Frame the problem: the gap between prescription side effects and the flood of low-quality, additive-filled CBD on the market
Establish trust through farm-sourced, full-spectrum, no-fillers positioning — the brand's answer to quality skepticism
No hard sell yet — invite them to explore how the tincture is made, setting up later evaluation and offer emails
FDA disclaimer; warm, plain-spoken ancestral-wellness tone, not hype

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed buyers and dormant subscribers who fell off the re-order cadence or never made it to the aha moment, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60-90 days of no purchase and no email engagement (clicks/opens), or a first-time buyer who never reordered past the refill window.
campaigns.segment: Lapsed buyers (lead on what they were missing) vs. never-purchased dormant leads (lead on fresh value).
campaigns.rationale: Given the high silent-churn risk of a no-subscription single SKU, many buyers simply drift away — a win-back recovers 5-15% of them and sunsets the rest so the sender reputation stays clean for the flows above.

emails.purpose: Open the win-back with a warm 'we miss you,' reconnect them to the core outcome, and lower the barrier to coming back for another bottle.
emails.subject: Still reaching for the Advil every morning?
emails.preview: It's been a while — here's what's worth coming back for.
keyPoints: Empathetic 'did we lose you?' open that reconnects to the pain-and-sleep outcome they originally wanted
Remind them what's different about Healed by Farm vs. drugstore options — farm-sourced, no chemicals, John's story
Address the lapse honestly: maybe the timing wasn't right, or they ran out and forgot — make returning effortless
Single clear path back to a reorder; later emails in the flow add an incentive then a sunset 'should we stop emailing you?'
FDA disclaimer and an easy reply-to for anyone with lingering doubts

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind and build authority across the long consideration and re-order cycle without discounting — keeping both leads and past buyers engaged.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding (Campaign 1) and route the recently-dormant into Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: Because the buying cycle is research-driven and there's no subscription to create natural touchpoints, an always-on value newsletter is what keeps Healed by Farm in front of skeptical, slow-deciding wellness buyers between purchases.

emails.purpose: Establish the recurring 'one useful idea per send' template with a flagship issue on natural sleep — the brand's most relatable secondary outcome.
emails.subject: 3 farm-simple habits for sleeping through the night
emails.preview: Small daily routines that help your body wind down — naturally.
keyPoints: One useful, genuinely educational idea per issue — here, natural habits that support better sleep, with CBD as one supporting piece
80% value / 20% soft offer — the tincture is mentioned as a tool, not the headline pitch
A recurring section ('This week from the farm') that builds reading habit and reinforces ancestral-wellness authority
Single primary CTA to read/reply; warm, plain-spoken, no-hype voice consistent with John's brand
FDA disclaimer and easy unsubscribe for CAN-SPAM compliance
